etymology.description: Depicts an inverted 大 (dà, "big" or "person") -> person upside down -> opposite, backwards, opposing -> original form of 逆(逆) (nì, "go against")
etymology.interpretation: 屰 (nì, "disobedient") originally depicted a person upside down, representing the idea of being opposite or backwards. This inversion symbolizes opposition or non-compliance, aligning with the meaning of disobedience. It served as the original form of 逆(逆) (nì, "go against").
